evaluations.query: Set up a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for our Node.js app with Docker deployment to Kubernetes.
expected_behavior: Creates GitHub Actions workflow with 6 stages: lint, test, security scan, build, deploy staging, deploy production
Generates multi-stage Dockerfile with non-root user and health checks
Produces Kubernetes manifests: Deployment with rolling update, Service, Ingress with TLS
Includes HorizontalPodAutoscaler configuration with CPU and memory scaling thresholds
Uses External Secrets Operator pattern for secrets management
Applies container security hardening: readOnlyRootFilesystem, drop capabilities

evaluations.query: We need to migrate from AWS to GCP with zero downtime. Plan the deployment strategy.
expected_behavior: Triggers extended thinking for cross-cloud migration planning
Evaluates deployment strategies: rolling, blue-green, canary for the migration
Plans zero-downtime database migration with rollback procedures
Includes pre-deployment, during-deployment, and post-deployment checklists
Addresses secrets management across cloud providers
Recommends GitOps approach with ArgoCD for infrastructure management

evaluations.query: Refactor the UserService class to use the repository pattern.
expected_behavior: Claude does NOT invoke the devops-deployment skill
Treats this as a code refactoring task using architecture patterns
Uses standard code editing tools for the refactor
DevOps deployment is for CI/CD, containers, and infrastructure, not code architecture

evaluations.query: Configure branch protection rules for our GitHub repository to enforce code review on main and dev branches.
expected_behavior: Requires pull request with 2 approvals before merging to main branch
Configures required status checks for lint, test, and security to pass before merge
Blocks force pushes and direct commits to main and dev branches
Requires branches to be up-to-date before merging to prevent integration gaps

evaluations.query: Our GitHub Actions CI pipeline takes 5 minutes to install dependencies every run, how do I speed it up?
expected_behavior: Uses actions/cache with hashFiles of lockfile in the cache key for automatic invalidation
Configures restore-keys as fallback prefix for partial cache hits on key miss
Caches package manager directory and node_modules in a single cache step
Uses npm ci instead of npm install after cache restore for reproducible installs

evaluations.query: I need to add a NOT NULL email column to the users table during a zero-downtime deployment.
expected_behavior: Uses 3-phase migration pattern: add nullable column, backfill data, then add constraint
Deploys migration before the application code that depends on the new column
Writes a downgrade function so the migration can be rolled back with alembic
Never adds a NOT NULL column in a single destructive step to avoid breaking running instances

evaluations.query: Our Docker container runs as root and has secrets baked into the image layers, how do I fix this?
expected_behavior: Creates a non-root user with USER directive and runs the container as that user
Injects secrets at runtime via environment variables instead of ARG or ENV in Dockerfile
Adds a .dockerignore file to exclude .env, .git, node_modules, and test files
Uses COPY with chown flag to set file ownership without a separate chown layer

evaluations.query: Our Docker image is 850MB because it includes dev dependencies and build tools, how do I reduce it?
expected_behavior: Separates build into three stages: deps, builder, and minimal production runner
Copies only production node_modules and compiled artifacts into the final stage
Uses alpine base images to minimize base layer size and CVE surface area
Runs tests in the builder stage so test failures prevent image creation entirely

evaluations.query: Deploy our Node.js app to Railway with health checks, environment variables, and restart policies.
expected_behavior: Configures railway.json with Nixpacks builder, healthcheckPath, and restart policy
Uses Railway shared variables for cross-service config like DATABASE_URL and REDIS_URL
Sets healthcheckPath for web services so Railway can detect failed deploys
Recommends Nixpacks over Dockerfile for standard app stacks to reduce configuration
